methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Seneca is a Series A-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector, having raised $60M to date. The broader sector includes eight private peers—among them Joby Aviation, Nuro, and Archer Aviation—though no public comparables exist. This limited and nascent peer universe constrains traditional benchmarking approaches.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable lens. Using total raised ($60M) as a baseline valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields $120M. The peer-median approach was not applied: while four same-sector peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or comparable valuation tier yielded insufficient overlap (fewer than three datapoints), making sector-wide medians unreliably broad for this analysis.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, centered at $120M. This range reflects modest sensitivity around the 2.0x step-up assumption. Key uncertainties stem from the sparse comparable universe—the sector remains early and heterogeneous in business models, financing patterns, and technical maturity. Any refinement would require additional same-stage or recent Series A exit data in autonomous systems. Until then, the stage-progression result anchors the analysis, though it carries higher execution risk than a multi-method consensus would.
